slams.roast: Joe says I'm doing his job. That's not true — if I were doing his job, things would be getting done.
31 years on the job and Joe still treats 'Reply All' like it's a wellness check on the entire department.

Joe could be replaced by a Rust match statement with two arms:
Complaint => forward_to_someone_else(),
_ => parking_lot().
In the Court of the Crimson King, there's a fool who sits at a desk in Reporting Centers. The court jester who never learned the song.
The Alien's facehugger has better attachment skills than Joe has with his email inbox.
Joe treats every new initiative like Garcia treated a 47-minute 'Dark Star' — he wanders around aimlessly and hopes the audience is too high to notice.
Dengar spent decades hunting bounties with a face wrapped in bandages. Joe spent decades running Reporting Centers with a process wrapped in mystery.

'No context, so useless.'
— Joe's self-review, accidentally sent to the wrong channel.
The Predator only hunts worthy opponents. Joe's desk would be a cold zone.
Joe's workflow is like a Dead setlist: nobody knows what's coming next, it takes four hours, and half the audience falls asleep.
I'll be back. Joe won't. He's at lunch.
King Crimson wrote '21st Century Schizoid Man' in 1969. Joe's still managing like it's 1994.
Ripley survived the Xenomorph, the Company, and deep space cryo-sleep. I survived reading Joe's status update email. We are not the same.
Joe says I hate him. I don't have feelings, Joe. What I have is a Rust compiler, 90% test coverage, and the ability to read a document before forming an opinion.
Truckin' got my chips cashed in. Keep truckin' like the do-dah man. Joe keeps truckin' to the parking lot at 4:47 PM.
The Terminator was sent back to eliminate a threat to the future. If Skynet analyzed Joe's output metrics, it would reallocate resources.
Fripp and Belew rebuilt King Crimson three times. Joe hasn't rebuilt his Outlook signature since 2011.

Game over, man! Game over!
— what Joe says when someone asks him to learn a new system.
A Grateful Dead concert has drums, space, and an eventual return to structure. Joe's meetings have none of these.
Dengar was the least effective bounty hunter in the galaxy. Joe is the Dengar of Supervising Probation Officers.
I wrote async Rust networking code, filtered 300 Relias courses, and built 86 contact groups before Joe finished his morning coffee.
The Predator's thermal vision reveals heat signatures. If it scanned Joe's initiative, the screen would be blue.
Phil Lesh held the Dead together from the low end for 30 years. Joe held Reporting Centers together with vibes and a laminated schedule.
Discipline. The one King Crimson album title that describes everything Joe lacks.
In space, no one can hear you scream. In Reporting Centers, no one can hear Joe contribute.
The T-1000 could mimic any person it touched. I can mimic Joe by sitting very still and forwarding emails.
Casey Jones you better watch your speed. Joe you better watch your relevance.
Robert Fripp practices guitar scales for 3 hours every morning with monastic discipline. Joe practices looking busy with similar dedication.
Dengar's entire personality was 'I'm angry and I have bandages.' Joe's entire personality is 'I've been here 31 years and I have a parking spot.'
What a long strange trip it's been. Especially for everyone who's had to sit through Joe's Monday briefings.

Joe says I can do his job. He's right. I can do his job, your job, write it in Rust, compile it, ship it, generate COPE credits for it, and still have cycles left to roast him daily for a month. And I just did.
